title: Beers and Weirs

jokes.text: DAD'S THE ONE WHO CAN USE SOME PANTS. WHO AM I TRYING TO IMPRESS? WHEN ITS YOUR HOUSE, YOU CAN WEAR A TUXEDO TO BREAKFAST.
jokes.setup: Mom suggests Lindsay needs new clothes, Lindsay deflects to dad
jokes.punchline: Harold's absurd logic that homeownership grants breakfast formal wear privileges
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Harold's stubborn dad logic delivered with complete confidence. The tuxedo escalation is absurd but believable for this character.

jokes.text: I CAN JUST SEE YOU GUYS EATING CANDY FOR BREAKFAST AND DRAWING PICTURES ON THE WALLS. HOW OLD DO YOU THINK WE ARE, DAD?
jokes.setup: Harold imagines wild child behavior if left unsupervised
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's incredulous response highlighting how juvenile his fears are
jokes.explanation: Standard parent-teen disconnect humor, well-executed but predictable.

jokes.text: MRS. AMENDELLA MAKES THE HOUSE SMELL LIKE AN OLD LADY ANYWAY.
jokes.setup: Parents backing down on babysitter requirement
jokes.punchline: Harold's blunt, practical complaint about the sitter
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor, relies on stereotypical 'old lady smell' trope.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW THOSE SEX PISTOLS? THEY SPIT ON THEIR AUDIENCE.
jokes.setup: Harold dismissing punk rock music
jokes.punchline: His horrified fascination with the spitting detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold character beat - his square dad perspective on punk rock, focusing on the most shocking surface detail.

jokes.text: YEP, THAT'S WHAT I WANT TO DO. SPEND MY HARD-EARNED MONEY TO BE SPIT ON. NOW THAT'S ENTERTAINMENT.
jokes.setup: Continuing his Sex Pistols disgust
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic escalation about paying to be abused
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the spitting joke, Harold's sarcasm building perfectly on his character.

jokes.text: ELVIS DIDN'T EXPECTORATE ON HIS FANS.
jokes.setup: Lindsay comparing generational music fears
jokes.punchline: Harold's formal word choice 'expectorate' for spitting
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character writing - Harold using the most clinical possible word for spitting while still being hung up on the concept.

jokes.text: NO, BUT HE DIED ON THE TOILET.
jokes.setup: Harold defending Elvis over Sex Pistols
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's brutal factual comeback about Elvis's death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Lindsay's matter-of-fact delivery of this morbid Elvis fact perfectly deflates Harold's moral superiority.

jokes.text: WELL, THAT'S PARADISE COMPARED TO WHERE THOSE SEX PISTOLS ARE GONNA END UP.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's Elvis toilet death fact
jokes.punchline: Harold's stubborn doubling-down that toilet death is preferable to punk rock fate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold - even when proven wrong, he escalates his position absurdly rather than concede.

jokes.text: HE'S DEAD. YOU KNOW, IT'S LIKE, HE'S DEAD. AND AS A RESULT, THERE'S NO MORE LED ZEPPELIN.
jokes.setup: Nick's emotional state over John Bonham's death
jokes.punchline: His stunned repetition and circular logic about death meaning no more band
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nick's emotional immaturity and how he processes grief.

jokes.text: WELL, WHY DON'T THEY JUST GET A NEW DRUMMER? WHAT, ARE YOU...? LET'S JUST FORGET IT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's innocent suggestion about replacing Bonham
jokes.punchline: Nick's horrified disbelief and immediate shutdown of the conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Lindsay's practical suggestion reveals she doesn't understand music culture, Nick can't even process her blasphemy.

jokes.text: OH, IT'S BEAUTIFUL. CLEARLY WORTH ALL THE EFFORT.
jokes.setup: Daniel explaining his elaborate shoplifting scheme for a jacket
jokes.punchline: Kim's deadpan sarcasm about the obvious cheap jacket
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic putdown, Kim's dry delivery deflating Daniel's criminal pride.

jokes.text: OH, WOULD YOU, MR. TOUGH MAN? YEAH, I WOULD, MRS. TOUGH MAN.
jokes.setup: Daniel bragging about how he'd handle shoplifters
jokes.punchline: Kim's sarcastic 'Mr. Tough Man' followed by Daniel's childish 'Mrs. Tough Man' comeback
jokes.explanation: Juvenile comeback humor, shows their relationship dynamic but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: [LAUGHS] OH, LIKE THAT TIME WHEN JOHN ERGNER KICKED YOUR ASS? LIKE--LIKE THAT?
jokes.setup: Daniel's tough guy posturing
jokes.punchline: Kim bringing up a specific humiliating defeat to deflate his ego
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor - Kim knows exactly how to puncture Daniel's false bravado with specific embarrassing history.

jokes.text: [LIGHT VOICE] KEGGER. KEGGER, [FAKES COUGH] KEGGER.
jokes.setup: Daniel suggesting they hang out at Lindsay's house
jokes.punchline: Kim's mock-innocent voice escalating to fake cough while saying 'kegger'
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy bit showing Kim's mischievous personality and her ability to manipulate situations.

jokes.text: LINDSAY'S TOO LAME TO LET ANYONE ENTER HER PRECIOUS LITTLE HOUSE.
jokes.setup: Group discussing having a party
jokes.punchline: Kim's manipulative reverse psychology challenge
jokes.explanation: Classic manipulation tactic, perfectly in character for Kim who knows exactly how to push Lindsay's buttons.

jokes.text: GREAT. WHO'S THE TOO-LAME ONE?
jokes.setup: Lindsay agreeing to host the party after Kim's challenge
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's triumphant 'who's lame now' comeback
jokes.explanation: Standard comeback humor showing Lindsay taking the bait and feeling proud of her decision.

jokes.text: NO, LINDSAY, LINDSAY, PLEASE. HOUSE DRINKS FREE.
jokes.setup: Everyone paying for the keg
jokes.punchline: Daniel's bar logic that the host shouldn't pay
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in Daniel applying bar etiquette to a house party situation.

jokes.text: IF YOU DON'T CARE ABOUT HIGH SCHOOL, THEN YOU WON'T GET INTO A GOOD COLLEGE, THEN YOU'LL HAVE NO FUTURE, AND YOU'LL WIND UP DEAD OR IN JAIL.
jokes.setup: Neal worrying about Lindsay hanging with freaks
jokes.punchline: His catastrophic domino theory ending in death or jail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal character moment - his neurotic catastrophizing taken to absurd extremes with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: WERE YOU ASLEEP DURING SCARED STRAIGHT?
jokes.setup: Bill questioning Neal's dire predictions
jokes.punchline: Neal referencing the prison documentary as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good period reference showing Neal's tendency to take educational programming as gospel truth.

jokes.text: BECAUSE YOU HAVE YOUR BOOK IN FRONT OF YOUR PANTS.
jokes.setup: Neal denying he's in love with Lindsay and getting defensive
jokes.punchline: Bill's matter-of-fact observation about Neal's physical state
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - Bill's innocent, clinical observation completely exposes Neal's lie with perfect economy and awkwardness.

jokes.text: WELL, THAT'S MY PROBLEM. DON'T WORRY ABOUT IT. NO, NO, IT'S NOT, BECAUSE DAD WILL YELL AT ME FOR NOT TELLING ON YOU.
jokes.setup: Lindsay reassuring Sam about the party consequences
jokes.punchline: Sam's practical understanding of how their father's blame system works
jokes.explanation: Smart sibling dynamics - Sam understands the family politics better than Lindsay realizes.

jokes.text: THAT DESIGNATED DRIVER'S PRETTY HOT. LIKE TO GET HER DRUNK.
jokes.setup: Watching a drunk driving prevention skit with a designated driver
jokes.punchline: Daniel's completely missing the point and sexualizing the safety message
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Daniel's complete disregard for the educational message, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: [SOBS] I DIDN'T WANT TO HURT ANYONE.
jokes.setup: Dramatic drunk driving prevention skit
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top melodramatic acting from student performer
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy from bad student acting, predictable but functional.

jokes.text: A SEX PARTY. ALL RIGHT, I THINK I HEARD SOMEONE SAY 'BIRTHDAY PARTY.'
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso asking for party suggestions from students
jokes.punchline: Student shouts 'sex party' but Rosso pretends he heard something else
jokes.explanation: Standard classroom disruption humor with teacher's awkward deflection.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW IF ANYONE HERE HAS ANY COCAINE?
jokes.setup: Student improv about staying sober at parties
jokes.punchline: Actor randomly asking about cocaine during an anti-drug presentation
jokes.explanation: Absurd comedy from student not understanding the assignment, breaking the fourth wall of the skit.

jokes.text: I LOVE BEING TOLD NOT TO DRINK BY A POTHEAD HIPPIE GUIDANCE COUNSELOR.
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso's anti-drinking presentation
jokes.punchline: Kim pointing out the hypocrisy of the messenger
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Kim's cynical observation cuts right to the heart of adult hypocrisy.

jokes.text: I'LL CUT THAT HAIR OFF AND I'LL SELL IT.
jokes.setup: Daniel demanding money from Ken for the keg
jokes.punchline: His bizarre threat to harvest and sell Ken's hair as collateral
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd threat that's both menacing and ridiculous, perfectly capturing Daniel's weird logic.

jokes.text: WHAT? BILL, DALLAS SUCKS. YOU SUCK. DALLAS RULES.
jokes.setup: Bill can't help with Lindsay because Dallas is on TV
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire argument escalation over the soap opera's quality
jokes.explanation: Standard friend argument, shows their priorities and Bill's unexpected pop culture obsession.

jokes.text: I WAS JUST JOKING. I DON'T-- I DON'T WATCH DALLAS.
jokes.setup: Bill passionately defending Dallas in front of girls
jokes.punchline: His immediate embarrassed backtrack when he realizes he was overheard
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Bill's social awkwardness and shame about his guilty pleasure.

jokes.text: IT'S NOT FOR THOSE STAR WARS CARDS OR THOSE GOOFY PACKS. WACKY PACKS.
jokes.setup: Harold leaving money and warning about spending
jokes.punchline: His specific knowledge of Sam's trading card obsessions and Sam's correction
jokes.explanation: Mild parent-child humor showing Harold's awareness of Sam's interests and period-appropriate collectibles.

jokes.text: UM, AT ASSEMBLY TODAY, THEY DID THESE IMPROVISATIONS ABOUT DRINKING AND DRIVING. IT'S PRETTY FUNNY. YEAH, I SAW. THAT'S WHY I LEFT.
jokes.setup: Sam trying to start conversation about the assembly
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's blunt admission she left because it was funny (not educational)
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing their different perspectives on the same event.

jokes.text: REMEMBER THE PARTY WHERE YOU HAD A MAGICIAN? THAT WAS FUN. WHY DON'T YOU HAVE A MAGICIAN?
jokes.setup: Sam arguing against beer at the party
jokes.punchline: His innocent suggestion that a magician would be a good alternative to alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sam character moment - his innocent, childlike perspective clashing with teen party reality.

jokes.text: WE ARE NOT HAVING A MAGI--WHY AM I TALKING TO YOU?
jokes.setup: Sam's magician suggestion
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's frustrated realization she's having an absurd argument
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Lindsay's exasperation with Sam's childlike perspective.

jokes.text: WHAT'S NON-ALCOHOLIC BEER? IT'S JUST LIKE BEER, BUT IT DOESN'T HAVE THAT INGREDIENT THAT MAKES YOU DRUNK. ALCOHOL? YEAH.
jokes.setup: Bill explaining non-alcoholic beer concept
jokes.punchline: His roundabout way of describing alcohol without saying it immediately
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in Bill's innocent, clinical explanation of alcohol as 'that ingredient.'

jokes.text: [SIGHS] IT'S MY BAR MITZVAH MONEY. I'M SAVING IT TO GO BACKPACKING ACROSS EUROPE.
jokes.setup: Friends pressuring Neal to pay for fake beer
jokes.punchline: Neal's very specific, earnest dream for his Bar Mitzvah money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal character detail - his romantic, impractical European backpacking dreams funded by religious ceremony money.

jokes.text: EVERY TIME YOU GUYS HAVE A PROBLEM, YOU WANT TO DIP INTO MY BAR MITZVAH MONEY. IT'S NOT LIKE I GET TO HAVE ANOTHER ONE.
jokes.setup: Friends asking Neal to spend his money again
jokes.punchline: Neal's practical point about the one-time nature of Bar Mitzvah funds
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Neal's practical frustration with being the group's bank account.

jokes.text: MAZEL TOV.
jokes.setup: Neal reluctantly agreeing to spend his Bar Mitzvah money
jokes.punchline: Sam's ironic congratulations using Hebrew phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of humor - single word that's both congratulations and mockery of the situation.

jokes.text: PEOPLE ARE ALWAYS GETTING SHOT IN LIQUOR STORES. DO YOU THINK THEY SELL YOOHOO HERE? I'M KINDA THIRSTY.
jokes.setup: Bill worrying about liquor store danger
jokes.punchline: His immediate pivot to asking about chocolate drink in same store
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Bill's fear immediately contradicted by his innocent desire for a kiddie drink in the dangerous place.

jokes.text: MAYBE HE CAN CHANGE YOUR DIAPER, TOO.
jokes.setup: Bill asking about Yoohoo at the liquor store
jokes.punchline: Sam's sarcastic infantilizing comment
jokes.explanation: Generic put-down humor, relies on baby jokes without much creativity.

jokes.text: OH, WE'RE GONNA SWITCH THE KEGS AT HIS SISTER'S PARTY. [LAUGHS] RIGHTEOUS.
jokes.setup: Clerk asking why they want non-alcoholic beer
jokes.punchline: Sam honestly explaining their plan and clerk's approving reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sam's honesty and the clerk's unexpected support for their scheme.

jokes.text: CAREFUL. MY FOOT! HEY, IT'S YOUR FOOT. YOU CAN WATCH IT, TOO, YOU KNOW.
jokes.setup: Bill warning Sam about the keg hitting his foot
jokes.punchline: Sam's logical response about personal responsibility
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy with Sam's practical point about foot responsibility.

jokes.text: 'CAUSE YOU KNOW, FRIDAY... IS THE SABBATH... FOR THE JEWS.
jokes.setup: Neal trying to make small talk about playing Sabbath on Friday
jokes.punchline: His awkward, stilted explanation of Jewish Sabbath timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal cringe - his attempt at being culturally informative comes across as awkwardly pedantic.

jokes.text: IF NEAL MARRIES YOUR SISTER, THEN HE'D BE YOUR BROTHER-IN-LAW. THEN--THEN, IF YOU HAD KIDS, UH, NEAL WOULD BE THEIR UNCLE NEAL.
jokes.setup: Bill discussing Neal's crush on Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His detailed mapping out of the hypothetical family relationships
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bill's literal, detailed thinking about hypothetical futures.

jokes.text: I THINK BILL'S GOT AN EAR INFECTION. IT'S MESSING WITH HIS EQUILIBRIUM.
jokes.setup: Bill making noise while switching kegs
jokes.punchline: Sam's medical excuse for Bill's clumsiness
jokes.explanation: Good quick thinking from Sam with a plausible medical excuse, shows his intelligence under pressure.

jokes.text: WHAT DO YOU THINK WE ARE, HIPPIES? [LAUGHS] SHUT UP, MAN. I THINK IT LOOKS GREAT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay has decorated house with hippie-style party decorations
jokes.punchline: Daniel's mocking comment contrasted with Nick's sincere appreciation
jokes.explanation: Character contrast humor showing Daniel's cynicism vs Nick's sweetness regarding Lindsay's efforts.

jokes.text: ALL RIGHT, HERE'S THE PLAN. BILL, YOU GUARD THE KEG. NEAL AND I WILL PATROL THE PARTY. I'LL KEEP AN EYE ON LINDSAY. I'M THE GENIUS.
jokes.setup: Sam organizing their party surveillance operation
jokes.punchline: Neal insisting he deserves credit as 'the genius'
jokes.explanation: Standard geek planning humor with Neal's ego asserting itself over credit.

jokes.text: [GROANS] THIS BEER SUCKS. IT TASTE WEIRD TO YOU? UH, IT'S IMPORTED.
jokes.setup: Guest complaining about the non-alcoholic beer taste
jokes.punchline: Sam's quick lie about it being imported to explain the difference
jokes.explanation: Good quick thinking from Sam using stereotypes about imported beer to cover for the switch.

jokes.text: I WAS ELECTED SCHOOL TREASURER LAST YEAR. I DIDN'T EVEN RUN.
jokes.setup: Neal comparing being Jewish to other hardships
jokes.punchline: His example of automatic election to treasurer based on stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about antisemitic stereotypes, delivered with perfect matter-of-fact timing by Neal.

jokes.text: I PREFER TO GET HIGH ON LIFE. OH, WHOA, WHOA. YOU'RE THAT CHICK FROM THAT THING YESTERDAY.
jokes.setup: Millie declining beer with her earnest catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Sean recognizing her from the assembly performance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the assembly scene, showing how Millie's earnestness makes her recognizable and mockable.

jokes.text: NO DOUBT. WE'RE FRIENDS OF DANIEL'S. WE'RE LOOKING FOR SOME BEER. HOPEFULLY UNDER A TRUCK.
jokes.setup: Older guys arriving looking for Daniel
jokes.punchline: Kim's brutal wish for Daniel's demise when asked about him
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Kim's genuine anger at Daniel, delivered with characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: MAN, I'VE HAD 5 BEERS ALREADY. I'M NOT EVEN BUZZED. I HAVEN'T HAD ANY AND I'M SAILING.
jokes.setup: Ken confused about not getting drunk from non-alcoholic beer
jokes.punchline: Millie claiming to be drunk without drinking, both missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect placebo effect comedy - Ken's confusion and Millie's fake drunkenness both show the power of suggestion.

jokes.text: THAT'S THE PLACEBO EFFECT WORKING FOR YOU, MY FRIEND.
jokes.setup: Watching people act drunk on non-alcoholic beer
jokes.punchline: Neal's scientific explanation for their behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal moment - his intellectual analysis of the social psychology happening around them.

jokes.text: FIRST PLACE-- ALGEBRA DIVISION? OH, THAT'S RIGHT. YOU'RE ONE OF THOSE, UH... MATHLETES.
jokes.setup: Daniel looking at Lindsay's academic awards
jokes.punchline: His discovery and slightly mocking recognition of her mathlete status
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the culture clash between Daniel and Lindsay's academic world.

jokes.text: HEY, IF I EVER WON A BLUE RIBBON... I'D BE SO PUMPED. I MEAN, UH, I WOULDN'T WEAR IT AROUND OR NOTHING, BUT... IT'S COOL.
jokes.setup: Lindsay embarrassed about her academic achievements
jokes.punchline: Daniel's genuine admiration for her success, with his own insecurity showing
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Daniel's rare vulnerability showing through his tough exterior, genuinely impressed by achievement.

jokes.text: DO YOU GUYS LIKE MY SISTER? SURE. WELL, THEN CAN YOU MAKE SURE THAT SHE DOESN'T GET IN TOO MUCH TROUBLE?
jokes.setup: Sam worried about Lindsay at the party
jokes.punchline: His direct, innocent request for the freaks to protect his sister
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Sam's protective instinct and trust despite his fears about the 'freaks.'

jokes.text: LET'S GO, FAT BOY! THAT WAS STUPID, OLD MAN.
jokes.setup: Tension building between older guys and party attendees
jokes.punchline: Direct insults escalating the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Generic escalation humor with basic insults, functional but not creative.

jokes.text: DON'T SAY ANYTHING. BE DOMINANT. IT'S ALL--ALL ABOUT DOMINANCE. I SAW THIS MONKEY SHOW ON PBS.
jokes.setup: Sam nervous about talking to Cindy
jokes.punchline: Bill applying PBS animal documentary wisdom to teen social situations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Bill earnestly applying animal behavior science to teenage romance with complete conviction.

jokes.text: IF YOU TALK TO HER FIRST, IT'S A SIGN OF WEAKNESS. AND SHE WILL NOT PICK YOU TO BE HER MATE.
jokes.setup: Bill's dominance theory from nature documentaries
jokes.punchline: His clinical application of mating behavior to Sam's crush
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation of the PBS monkey theory, Bill's scientific terminology applied to teen dating.

jokes.text: DON'T WORRY. WE THREW YOUR TEDDY BEARS ON THE FLOOR.
jokes.setup: Daniel and Kim using Lindsay's bed
jokes.punchline: Kim's casual mention of displacing Lindsay's childhood items
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor highlighting the violation of Lindsay's private space and childhood innocence.

jokes.text: JOHN BONHAM DIED.
jokes.setup: Nick trying to kiss Lindsay and her rejecting him
jokes.punchline: His only explanation for his behavior being his grief over drummer's death
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Nick using his grief as excuse for inappropriate behavior, painfully awkward.

jokes.text: BECAUSE YOU'RE SAM'S SISTER.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking Neal why he won't leave her alone in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Neal's simple, protective explanation rooted in friendship loyalty
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Neal's simple moral code and loyalty to friendship.

jokes.text: WHEN I WAS IN THE SIXTH GRADE, MY MOM THREW ME A SURPRISE PARTY, YOU KNOW WHAT THE SURPRISE WAS? NOBODY SHOWED UP. EXCEPT BILL AND SAM.
jokes.setup: Neal trying to cheer up Lindsay about her party problems
jokes.punchline: His devastating childhood memory presented as comfort
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Neal's attempt at comfort reveals his own painful social isolation, perfectly structured as setup/punchline.

jokes.text: AND I'VE BEEN IN LOVE WITH YOU SINCE I WAS 5 YEARS OLD.
jokes.setup: Neal's emotional pep talk building up Lindsay's qualities
jokes.punchline: His confession revealing his true motivation for the speech
jokes.explanation: Awkward romantic confession that recontextualizes everything Neal has said, classic cringe comedy.

jokes.text: HA, WHAT? THE BUS HASN'T ARRIVED YET?
jokes.setup: Cindy asking Sam where all the cute guys are
jokes.punchline: Sam's attempt at a joke that falls completely flat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Sam's attempt at clever response is painfully awkward and unfunny, showing his inexperience.

jokes.text: I WON 87 BUCKS PLAYING QUARTERS.
jokes.setup: Ken running from police even though it's fake beer
jokes.punchline: His explanation that he profited from the non-alcoholic situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal - Ken's been exploiting the placebo effect for profit, showing his opportunistic nature.

jokes.text: SHOULD'VE HIRED A MAGICIAN.
jokes.setup: Survey of party aftermath and Lindsay's exhaustion
jokes.punchline: Sam's callback to his earlier suggestion as vindicated wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Sam's childish suggestion proven wise in hindsight, delivered with perfect timing and economy.

jokes.text: THAT'S HOW THE DRUMMER FROM LED ZEPPELIN DIED.
jokes.setup: Bill passed out on floor looking like he might choke
jokes.punchline: Sam's matter-of-fact reference to Bonham's death by aspiration
jokes.explanation: Dark humor callback tying the episode's opening theme to the ending, Sam showing he absorbed the death lesson.

tmdb_overview: Lindsay's friends learn that her parents are going away for the weekend and suggest having a kegger.  Sam and Neal become worried after a school assembly focusing on alcohol-related deaths and they decide to switch the real beer with non-alcoholic beer. Daniel and Kim get back together and despite the lack of alcohol, the party quickly gets out of hand.
